page: Amazon Web Services c5ad.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AMD EPYC 7R32 processor. It features 64 virtual CPUs (32 physical cores with hyperthreading) running at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is built on the AWS Nitro System hypervisor and includes 128 GB of DDR4 RAM operating at 3 GHz, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-memory ratio.

This instance is equipped with 2400 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, offering high-speed local storage directly attached to the host. Networking capabilities include a baseline bandwidth of 20 Gbps, facilitating efficient data transfer for distributed workloads. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with Passmark CPU, memory, and database operations scoring in the top 25%. Geekbench ray tracing also demonstrates strong performance. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the bottom 10%. Multi-core stress-ng, memory bandwidth, and LLM inference for smaller models fall within average ranges, while large-model text generation is weak.

The c5ad.16xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-intensive workloads that benefit from AMD's resource density and built-in local NVMe storage. It is highly suitable for multi-threaded applications, database operations, software compilation, and batch processing. It is less optimal for workloads heavily reliant on single-threaded performance.
description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs (32 physical cores) powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it includes 128 GB of DDR4 RAM and 2400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show strong results in Passmark CPU, memory, and database operations, alongside strong Geekbench ray tracing performance. However, single-core processing represents a performance tradeoff, scoring in the bottom 10% on stress-ng. This instance provides a cost-effective option for multi-threaded, compute-heavy workloads that require high-speed local storage, such as database operations, software compilation, and batch processing, while avoiding workloads dependent on high single-thread performance.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 2400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, offering strong multi-core and database performance with a single-core tradeoff.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.16xlarge features 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 2400 GB NVMe SSD storage for compute-heavy and database workloads.
tagline: A compute-optimized instance featuring sixty-four AMD EPYC vCPUs, high-speed local NVMe storage, and strong multi-core performance for database and parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 128 GB DDR4 RAM powered by AMD EPYC 7R32
2400 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Strong performance in database operations and multi-core benchmarks
Tradeoff of lower single-core CPU performance
Best suited for multi-threaded applications, databases, and software compilation
